concerns.text: Post-hoc power analysis is generally considered less informative than a priori power analysis for study design. For exploratory work this is acceptable, but the plan should clarify whether power analysis will be used to justify sample size adequacy or only to interpret results post-hoc.

5-fold cross-validation is specified but stratification strategy is unclear. For materials data, similar compounds may cluster together; random splits could leak information. Consider specifying stratified CV by material class or leave-one-cluster-out if structural similarity groups exist.

VIF > 5 triggers 'descriptive-only' flag but the plan does not specify whether correlated predictors should be removed, combined, or retained with caveats. This affects model interpretability and should be explicit.

Spec states 'experimental thermal conductivity values from curated literature datasets' but research.md notes 'Materials Project thermal properties endpoint primarily provides DFT-calculated thermal conductivity values'. This ambiguity should be resolved before implementation to ensure data source matches scientific intent.
